promo_headline: Marketing day. Receipts from Lenny, Chen, and a Shopify case.
whats_news: Lenny: a 6-month report on AI in PM workflows
Andrew Chen: the metrics that broke this year
Shopify: a Sidekick case study from a $20M store
Every: an essay on writing with AI memory

digest.title: Receipts and rewrites for the marketing layer.
digest.body: Thursday is for marketers. Lenny publishes a 6-month report on what changed in PM workflows, Andrew Chen calls out the legacy growth metrics that broke this year, Shopify drops a real Sidekick case study, Cloudflare finally prices streaming caches, and Every writes about memory-aware writing. None of it is hype — all of it is operator content.

items.headline: Lenny: 6 months of AI in PM workflows
items.deck: Lenny's newsletter published a six-month report on which AI patterns stuck in real PM workflows. This matters because most PM tooling reviews are pre-launch theater, and a six-month retrospective is the rarer signal.

items.headline: Andrew Chen: the growth metrics that broke this year
items.deck: Andrew Chen names five legacy growth metrics that became misleading once agents started doing onboarding. This matters because the dashboards every growth team uses are now lying in ways the team has not noticed.

items.headline: Shopify Sidekick case study: a $20M store
items.deck: Shopify published a case study of a $20M store running Sidekick across support and CRM. This matters because mid-market case studies are the unit of truth that SMB buyers actually read.

items.headline: Cloudflare prices streaming caches
items.deck: Cloudflare priced AI Gateway streaming caches at a flat per-million-token rate. This matters because the price point sets the floor for what chat-product margins look like for the rest of the year.

items.headline: Every: writing with AI memory
items.deck: Every published a long essay on what changes when an AI writing partner remembers across sessions. This matters because cross-session memory is the feature that makes AI feel less like a tool and more like a collaborator.

items.headline: Diff your growth dashboard against Chen's broken-metrics list.
items.why: You read the post today; you have not yet adjusted. The adjustment takes 30 minutes if done now.
items.try: Set a 30-min timer. Open your North Star + 5 secondary metrics. Mark which of Chen's critiques applies to each.

items.headline: Read the Lenny report. Pick one pattern to copy this quarter.
items.why: The six-month signal compresses six months of trial and error into one read; the savings are highest the day it ships.
items.try: Set a 45-min timer. Read fully. Star one pattern. Add it to next sprint's top of backlog.
